label: Phase 7 live portfolio
decision_reason: Best current paper baseline because it has the strongest 2024-2025 holdout result (+38.68% on 175 races). BEL is dormant here, so the active holdout is effectively the OP+CD portfolio.
operational_read: Use as the primary paper-trade basket if Cole wants one frozen portfolio today.
caution: 2024 was basically flat (+0.37%), so this is still volatile even though the two-year holdout is strongest overall.
note: Frozen 3-rule baseline. BEL contributes zero 2024-2025 races, so the current holdout is effectively the OP+CD legs.

label: Phase 8 frozen portfolio
decision_reason: Useful challenger, but it underperformed Phase 7 on holdout (+21.45% vs +38.68%) despite adding more mined rules and more weak legs.
operational_read: Track as a shadow basket, not as the default, until it beats Phase 7 on more forward data.
caution: Its better walk-forward headline is not enough to offset the weaker current holdout and the negative holdout legs inside the basket.
note: Frozen 7-rule Phase 8 basket. Useful as the headline multi-track challenger, but still full-history-mined before this replay.

label: Train-only yearly selector
decision_reason: Most honest validation benchmark, not the best live default. Its walk-forward ROI is still valuable context (+22.46% on 470 races), but its current 2024-2025 holdout is only +14.36% on 65 races.
operational_read: Keep as the honest benchmark for reports and sanity checks, not as the daily operating recipe.
caution: Some historical folds used the old BEL bridge candidate, so it should stay a benchmark artifact rather than a clean deployment rulebook.
note: The current honest selector baseline from walk_forward_validation.py. Historical context includes the pre-existing BEL bridge candidate in some earlier folds, but no new aliasing is added here.
